questions.q: Who were Duryodhana's parents?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana was the firstborn of the blind king Dhritarashtra and the blindfolded queen Gandhari.

questions.q: What was Duryodhana's position among the hundred Kauravas?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana was the eldest of Gandhari's hundred sons and led them all.

questions.q: Which weapon was Duryodhana's particular specialty?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana was counted among the greatest mace fighters of his age.

questions.q: Who trained Duryodhana in the art of mace fighting?
questions.explanation: Balarama, Krishna's elder brother, taught the mace to both Duryodhana and Bhima, and prized Duryodhana as his finest student.

questions.q: Whom did Duryodhana crown king of Anga at the tournament of princes?
questions.explanation: By crowning the mocked charioteer's son a king on the spot, Duryodhana won Karna's lifelong loyalty.

questions.q: Which uncle guided Duryodhana's schemes against the Pandavas?
questions.explanation: Shakuni, his mother's brother, whispered in Duryodhana's ear and rolled the dice for him.

questions.q: How much land did Duryodhana finally refuse to grant the Pandavas?
options: Even land enough for a needle's point
Half the kingdom they once ruled
All territory east of the Ganga
questions.explanation: He declared he would not spare even as much land as the point of a needle, making war certain.

questions.q: Who killed Duryodhana at the end of the great war?
questions.explanation: Bhima brought him down in the final mace duel, fulfilling his old vow.

questions.q: Which fatal blow brought Duryodhana down in his last duel?
options: A sword thrust to the heart
A spear cast into his chest
A mace strike that smashed his thighs
questions.explanation: Bhima's mace shattered Duryodhana's thighs, the one mortal weakness in his body.

questions.q: By what name did those who loved Duryodhana call him?
questions.explanation: His well-wishers called him Suyodhana, the fine fighter, softening the name's harsher meaning.

questions.q: What omen accompanied Duryodhana's birth?
options: An eclipse darkened the noon sun
questions.explanation: Jackals howled and asses brayed at his birth, and the wise read doom in the omens.

questions.q: Who counselled Dhritarashtra to abandon the infant Duryodhana?
questions.explanation: Vidura and the wise warned that the child would destroy the line, but the king's love refused.

questions.q: What did young Duryodhana do to Bhima at Pramanakoti?
options: Poisoned his food and threw him into the Ganga
Locked him inside a burning storehouse
Loosed venomous serpents into his bed
Pushed him from the palace ramparts
questions.explanation: Bhima survived the poison and the river, returning from the naga realms stronger than before.

questions.q: Which agent built Duryodhana's lac palace trap at Varanavata?
questions.explanation: Purochana raised the flammable house of lac and ghee, and burned in it himself.

questions.q: What humiliation befell Duryodhana in Maya's palace at Indraprastha?
options: He was barred at the gates by guards
He mistook a pool for solid floor and fell in
His crown was knocked off by a low archway
He lost his way and missed the royal feast
questions.explanation: Deceived by the hall of illusions, he fell into water he took for crystal, and the laughter that followed fed his envy.

questions.q: Who took Duryodhana captive during the Ghosha Yatra cattle expedition?
questions.explanation: The gandharva king Chitrasena captured him, and the exiled Pandavas freed him on Yudhishthira's orders.

questions.q: What did Duryodhana resolve to do after the Pandavas rescued him from the gandharvas?
options: Starve himself to death in shame
questions.explanation: Humiliated by owing his life to his enemies, he vowed to fast unto death until his allies talked him out of it.

questions.q: Offered a choice by Krishna before the war, what did Duryodhana pick?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana gladly took the Narayani Sena, leaving the unarmed Krishna to Arjuna.

questions.q: What did Duryodhana attempt during Krishna's peace embassy?
options: To seize and imprison the envoy
To bribe him with half the kingdom
To challenge him to a dice game
To poison him at the welcome feast
questions.explanation: When Duryodhana moved to capture the ambassador, Krishna revealed his cosmic form to the court.

questions.q: Which son of Duryodhana was killed by Abhimanyu at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu slew Duryodhana's son Lakshmana on the thirteenth day, sealing the order to trap the boy.

questions.q: Where did Duryodhana hide after his armies were destroyed?
options: In a mountain cave at Himavat
Beneath the waters of Lake Dvaipayana
questions.explanation: Using his power over water, he solidified the lake around himself to cool his burning body.

questions.q: Who raged at Bhima's below-the-belt blow in Duryodhana's final duel?
questions.explanation: Balarama, guru of both fighters, raised his plough in fury until Krishna calmed him.

questions.q: Why were Duryodhana's thighs vulnerable to Bhima despite Gandhari's gaze?
options: Her gaze weakened after years of blindfolding
A rishi's curse rested upon his thighs
He had covered his loins before facing her
Bhima's mace bore a divine blessing
questions.explanation: In the widely told episode, Krishna shamed him into wearing a loincloth, so the gaze never touched his thighs.

questions.q: Whom did Duryodhana appoint as his fourth army commander?
questions.explanation: After Bhishma, Drona, and Karna had fallen, Shalya led the Kaurava host on the eighteenth morning.

questions.q: How many akshauhinis fought under Duryodhana's banner at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana fielded eleven akshauhinis against the Pandavas' seven.

questions.q: Where was Duryodhana's final mace duel with Bhima fought?
options: On the banks of the Ganga at Hastinapura
On the dueling grounds of Dwarka
questions.explanation: The duel took place at sacred Samantapanchaka, which let the fallen prince claim a blessed death.

questions.q: What signal reminded Bhima to strike Duryodhana on the thighs?
options: Sahadeva pointed to the setting sun
questions.explanation: The thigh-slap signal is the widely told version; in the Shalya Parva it is Arjuna who gives it at Krishna's hint.

questions.q: Whom did the dying Duryodhana anoint as commander for one last strike?
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama, named his final commander, led the night raid on the sleeping Pandava camp.

questions.q: Where does Yudhishthira find Duryodhana when he reaches the afterlife?
options: Enthroned in heaven among the gods
Reborn as a beggar on earth
questions.explanation: Having died a kshatriya's death on holy ground, Duryodhana sits radiant in heaven, to Yudhishthira's dismay.

questions.q: Which grand sacrifice did Duryodhana perform while the Pandavas were in exile?
questions.explanation: Told the Rajasuya was impossible while Yudhishthira lived, Duryodhana held the Vaishnava yajna instead.
